w:r.w:t: Professional Networking Platform for Education, Research & Institutions
This section tracks the revision history of this Business Requirement Document to maintain a clear audit trail of changes, approvals, and authorship.

Link and Learn is a web-based professional networking platform purpose-built for the education, research, and institutional sector in India. It brings together students, faculty, researchers, institutions, government bodies, NGOs, funding agencies, and vendors within a single unified ecosystem.

Modelled on the professional networking paradigm popularised by LinkedIn, Link and Learn extends that foundation with domain-specific capabilities: verified academic credentials, research publication sharing, PhD and faculty recruitment workflows, vendor procurement marketplaces, and event management — all under a role-based access architecture that distinguishes between personal users and institutional accounts.

The platform is designed to be mobile-first and responsive, serving users across Android, iOS, and the web. Revenue is generated through premium institutional memberships, targeted advertising, vendor listings, and recruitment posting fees.

The education and research sector in India currently lacks a dedicated, integrated platform for professional networking and institutional collaboration. Existing general-purpose platforms (LinkedIn, Facebook, WhatsApp groups) are not designed for the specific workflows of academic institutions, researchers, NGOs, and government agencies. This creates the following critical gaps:
Researchers and faculty have no central, verified space to publish and discover academic work, collaborate on grants, or find peer reviewers.

Institutions managing recruitment for faculty, PhD positions, and research roles rely on fragmented channels — email circulars, portals, and physical notice boards — resulting in slow, inefficient hiring.
Schools and colleges procuring facility items (furniture, lab equipment, infrastructure) have no structured marketplace to discover and transact with verified vendors, leading to opaque procurement.
Academic credentials cannot be verified digitally in real time, creating friction in hiring and admissions processes.
Events, conferences, and seminars are announced through uncoordinated channels with no integrated registration or attendance management system.
Personal contact numbers of vendors and institutional staff are exposed during procurement inquiries, creating privacy and tracking challenges.
The primary objectives of the Link and Learn platform are:
Create a unified professional networking space for personal and institutional users across the education and research ecosystem.
Enable verified academic profiles with built-in resume generation and degree certificate validation.
Streamline recruitment for faculty, PhD, and research positions through structured job postings and automated candidate matching notifications.
Provide a vendor marketplace for institutional procurement with masked contact routing to protect privacy.
Support research collaboration through journals, article publishing, and AI-powered content authenticity detection.
Deliver a complete event management system covering creation, registration, notifications, and attendance.
Generate sustainable revenue through advertisements, premium accounts, vendor listings, and recruitment fees.
Ensure data privacy, scalability, and compliance throughout the platform architecture.
The following modules and capabilities are within the defined scope of the Link and Learn platform for the initial release:
Personal login for students, researchers, faculty, and job seekers.
Institutional login for schools, colleges, universities, government research agencies, NGOs, and funding agencies.
Role-based access control (RBAC) distinguishing personal users, institutional admins, and platform admins.
Institutional group profiles with the ability for personal users to link to their institution.
LinkedIn-style profile pages with bio, skills, education, experience, and publications.
Professional post feed with like, comment, and share functionality.
Connection and follow system between users and institutions.
Built-in resume creator with downloadable PDF output generated from profile data.
Degree certificate upload and validation workflow with admin review and verified badge.
Reference checking: connection-based endorsements with AI-assisted credibility scoring.
Rich-text editor for publishing journals and articles.
AI-powered detection for AI-generated content, plagiarism, and fake profile flagging.
Job and vacancy postings for schools, colleges, and research institutions.
Call-for-applications workflow for PhD programmes, faculty positions, and research roles.
Automated push notifications to matching candidates based on profile criteria.
Event creation, posting, and management by institutions and personal users.
Online registration with QR-code-based ticket generation.
Automated reminders and notifications for registered attendees.
In-app one-to-one and group messaging with real-time delivery.
Call masking: vendor/institution direct numbers are hidden; calls route through the platform number for privacy and analytics.
Marketplace for institutions to post procurement requirements (furniture, lab equipment, infrastructure items).
Vendor listing and discovery with product/service catalogue.
Facility management dashboard for institutions to track vendors, orders, and maintenance requests.
Targeted advertisement placements for institutions, vendors, funding agencies, and recruiters.
Sponsored posts and banner ad management.
Real-time push notifications for job alerts, event reminders, message alerts, and recruitment matches.
The following are explicitly excluded from the initial release of Link and Learn:
Native mobile applications (Android/iOS). The initial release is a responsive React web application. Native apps are deferred to a subsequent phase.
Direct telephony integration (live call masking). The call masking feature will be designed in the UI but will not be connected to a live telephony API (e.g., Exotel, Twilio) in v1.0.
Payment gateway integration. Premium subscriptions and vendor transaction payments are deferred to v2.0.
Government API integration (e.g., DigiLocker, NAD) for automated degree verification. v1.0 uses manual upload and admin review.
AI model training or hosting on proprietary infrastructure. AI features will consume third-party APIs (e.g., Hugging Face).
Multi-language support. The initial platform will be in English only.
Video conferencing or live event streaming.
Designs, builds, and deploys the platform
Students, faculty, researchers using the platform daily
Manage institution profiles, post jobs and events
Moderate content, approve institutions, validate certificates
Place targeted ads and sponsored content

The system shall support two distinct login types: Personal (individual users) and Institutional (organizations). Each type shall have a separate registration flow, profile schema, and access permissions.
Users can register with email/password. Institutional accounts require admin approval before activation. JWT tokens are issued on login. Role-based routes are enforced across the application.

Personal users shall have a LinkedIn-style profile with bio, skills, education history, work experience, publications, and certificates. The platform shall auto-generate a downloadable PDF resume from profile data on demand.
Profile is editable in real time. Resume PDF is generated in-browser and reflects current profile data. At least two resume templates are available.

Users shall be able to upload degree certificates for verification. Platform admins review uploaded documents and approve or reject them. Approved certificates display a verified badge on the user profile.
Upload supports PDF and image formats. Admin panel shows pending verification queue. Verified badge appears on profile within 48 hours of approval.
Users and institutions shall be able to publish journals and research articles using a rich-text editor. Articles are categorised, tagged, and discoverable via search.
Rich-text editor supports headings, lists, links, and image embeds. Articles are indexed and searchable by title, author, and tags. Published articles display on the author profile and in the feed.

The platform shall integrate an AI content detection tool that flags articles and journal submissions likely to be AI-generated, plagiarised, or from fake profiles. Flagged content is sent to admin review.

Detection API (e.g., Hugging Face) is called on article submission. A confidence score and flag are stored against each article. Flagged content is hidden pending review. Results are audit-able by admins.

Institutions shall be able to post job vacancies and call-for-applications (PhD, faculty, research positions). The system shall automatically match postings to eligible candidates based on profile criteria and push notifications to them.

Posting form captures role type, eligibility criteria, deadline, and department. Candidates matching the criteria receive a push notification within 1 hour of posting. Application status is trackable by both parties.
Users and institutions shall be able to create, post, and manage events. Attendees can register online and receive a QR-code-based ticket. Automated reminders are sent before the event.
Event creation form includes title, date/time, location, description, and capacity. QR ticket is generated on registration. Reminder notifications are sent 24 hours and 1 hour before the event.

The platform shall provide real-time in-app messaging between users. For phone-based contact with vendors and institutions, the platform shall mask direct numbers — calls are routed through the platform company number to protect privacy and enable analytics.
Messages deliver in real time. Direct phone numbers are never exposed in the UI. Call routing UI shows platform number only. (Live telephony integration is a v2.0 feature.)

Institutions shall be able to post procurement requirements for facility items. Vendors register, list their products/services, and are matched to relevant institution requirements. A facility management dashboard allows institutions to track orders, vendor interactions, and maintenance.
Requirement postings include item type, quantity, budget range, and deadline. Vendors can submit quotes directly. Dashboard shows open, in-progress, and completed procurement tasks.
The platform shall support targeted advertising for institutions, vendors, funding agencies, and recruiters. Ad formats include sponsored posts in the feed and banner placements.

Advertisers can submit an ad with target audience parameters (user type, institution type, location). Ads are displayed to matching users. Impression and click analytics are available to the advertiser.
Page load time must not exceed 3 seconds on a standard 4G connection for 95% of requests.
The system must support at least 10,000 concurrent users and 1,000 registered institutions at launch without performance degradation.
All data in transit must be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher. Passwords must be hashed using bcrypt. JWT tokens must expire within 24 hours.
Degree certificates, personal contact numbers, and reference data must be stored with access restricted to authorized roles only. Direct numbers must never be exposed in the UI.
The platform must maintain 99.5% uptime measured monthly, excluding scheduled maintenance windows communicated 48 hours in advance.
The web application must be fully responsive across mobile (360px+), tablet (768px+), and desktop (1024px+) viewports.
All AI detection results must be stored with a timestamp, model version, confidence score, and reviewer decision for audit purposes.

The front end must be built exclusively with ReactJS. PHP is the recommended backend for authentication, business logic, and server-side operations. MySQL (SQL) is the recommended database for all platform data storage.
The project is divided into three delivery phases. Each phase delivers a testable increment of the platform.
Auth (personal + institutional), profile pages, professional feed, journals & articles, PHP/MySQL backend setup
Job postings, events & registration, vendor connector, in-app messaging, auto resume PDF generator
AI content detection, degree validation, reference checking, push notifications, admin panel, advertisement module
The following resources are required to deliver the Link and Learn platform within the defined scope and timeline.
Functional and UI testing per sprint
Authentication, Relational Database, File Storage, Push Notifications (via third-party service)
Core application framework and build tooling
Server-side logic for AI detection, cert validation
The front end must be built exclusively with ReactJS as mandated by the project brief. No other JavaScript framework is permitted.
The project must be delivered within a 12-week internship timeline across three phases.
The initial release is a responsive web application only. Native mobile apps (Android/iOS) are out of scope for v1.0.
Live telephony integration for call masking is deferred to v2.0 due to cost and complexity. The UI will be designed but will not connect to a live telephony API.
Payment processing is out of scope for v1.0. Premium subscriptions and vendor transactions will be introduced in a later phase.
The platform will be in English only for the initial release.
AI detection features are dependent on third-party API availability and rate limits (Hugging Face).
A PHP + MySQL backend will be used as the primary server-side stack, hosted on a web server for v1.0.
The project supervisor will be available for weekly milestone reviews and will provide sign-off within 3 business days of each deliverable submission.
Degree certificate validation in v1.0 is a manual process: users upload documents and a platform admin reviews and approves them. No government API integration is assumed.
The reference checking algorithm in v1.0 operates on connection-based endorsements. Advanced AI scoring may be introduced in a later phase.
Users are assumed to have a modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari — latest two versions) and a stable internet connection.
Institutional accounts that require verification will be approved by the platform admin manually during the early launch period.
The Link and Learn platform v1.0 will be considered successfully delivered when the following criteria are met and verified:
Both personal and institutional login types are functional with RBAC enforced.
QA login testing with both account types
A user can create a profile, add all sections, and download a correctly formatted PDF resume.
An institution can post a job vacancy and a matching user receives a push notification.
Functional test with matching and non-matching profiles
An event can be created, a user registers and receives a QR-code ticket, and a reminder notification fires.
An article flagged as AI-generated by the detection API is withheld and appears in the admin review queue.
Submit a known AI-generated article and verify the flag
A degree certificate uploaded by a user is approved by an admin and a verified badge appears on the profile.
Real-time messages between two users are delivered without a page reload.
Two-device messaging test with delivery time measurement
Vendor direct numbers are not visible anywhere in the platform UI.
UI audit across all vendor-facing pages
The application is fully usable on mobile (360px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1280px) viewports.
Responsive testing across all three breakpoints
All Phase 1, 2, and 3 modules are functional and demonstrated to the project supervisor.
Final platform demo and supervisor sign-off

Link and Learn operates on a role-based access architecture that distinguishes between eight distinct user roles across two login categories — Personal and Institutional. Each role receives a tailored experience: only the features, data, and actions relevant to that role are surfaced.
Undergraduate, postgraduate, or doctoral students enrolled in institutions
Academic staff and independent researchers who publish work, collaborate on grants, and recruit
Educational institutions with admin accounts managed by designated institutional administrators
Central and state government research agencies, councils, and laboratories
Non-governmental organizations and funding agencies that publish grants and manage events
Suppliers and service providers of furniture, lab equipment, and educational materials
Internal operators responsible for content moderation, account verification, and oversight
Institutions, vendors, and publishers who place targeted advertisements on the platform
The table below maps each functional requirement to the roles that interact with it.
Undergraduate, postgraduate, or doctoral students enrolled in institutions. The largest user segment on the platform.
Create a LinkedIn-style academic profile; generate downloadable PDF resume from profile data
Upload certificates for admin verification; receive a verified badge upon approval
View, like, comment, and share posts from connections and followed institutions
Browse and apply for PhD programmes, research roles, and student-relevant job postings
Discover, register for events, receive QR-code tickets and automated reminders
One-to-one and group in-app messaging; masked contact routing for vendor queries
Connect with peers, faculty, and researchers; follow institutions
Browse and read published journals and research articles
Cannot post institutional job vacancies or procurement requirements
Cannot approve or reject certificates (admin-only action)
Cannot access platform admin panel or moderation tools
Register with email/password → select 'Student' personal account type
Complete profile: bio, education, skills, experience sections
Upload degree certificate → awaits admin verification (≤ 48 hrs)
Connect with peers and follow institutions → feed populates
Browse job/PhD listings → receive push notifications for matching roles
Register for events → receive QR ticket + reminders
Academic staff, postdoctoral researchers, and independent researchers who publish work, collaborate on grants, and recruit.
Full access to all features available to students
Publish research articles using the rich-text editor with categorisation and tagging
Submitted articles automatically scanned for AI-generated content and plagiarism
Provide connection-based endorsements; AI-assisted credibility scoring on profile
Apply for and track status of applications for faculty and research positions
Search and connect with peers working in related research areas
Receive notifications for relevant funding calls posted by NGOs/government bodies
Cannot post institutional job vacancies (institution account required)
Cannot manage institutional procurement or vendor interactions
Build full academic profile including publications and research interests
Publish journal/article → AI detection runs automatically on submission
Engage with research community via feed, comments, and messaging
Receive notifications for grant calls and PhD supervision opportunities
Provide and receive reference endorsements through connection network
Educational institutions with admin accounts managed by designated institutional administrators.
Public profile page showing institution details, departments, faculty, and verified badge
Post faculty, staff, and research role vacancies; auto-match to eligible candidates
Publish PhD programme openings with eligibility criteria; push notifications sent to matching students
Create and manage institutional events, handle online registration, issue QR tickets
Post facility procurement needs (furniture, lab equipment); receive vendor quotes
Track open, in-progress, and completed procurement tasks and vendor interactions
Communicate with vendors, applicants, and event registrants via masked contact routing
Personal users affiliated with the institution can link their profile to the institution page
Institutional account requires platform admin approval before activation
Cannot approve or validate degree certificates (platform admin only)
Cannot directly expose staff contact numbers in the UI
Payment for premium features deferred to v2.0
Register institutional account → submit for platform admin approval
Upon approval: complete institution profile, add departments and branding
Post faculty/PhD vacancies → system auto-notifies matching candidates
Create events → manage registrations and QR ticket issuance
Post procurement requirements → receive and evaluate vendor quotes
Track all procurement and HR activities via facility management dashboard
Central and state government research agencies, councils, and laboratories participating in the academic ecosystem.
Full access to all features available to school/college institutional accounts
Post interdisciplinary research project calls open to faculty and researchers nationwide
Publish grant calls with eligibility criteria, budget range, and application deadlines
Share official research advisories, reports, and policy documents to the platform feed
Manage national conferences, symposia, and workshops with high-capacity registration
Account requires platform admin approval and manual verification of government affiliation
Cannot transact payments through the platform in v1.0
Live telephony call masking deferred to v2.0
Register → select 'Government Research Body' institutional type
Platform admin manually verifies government affiliation before activation
Publish grant announcements and collaboration calls
Post large-scale events; manage registrations and QR-based attendance
Engage with researchers through verified institutional messaging
Non-governmental organizations and private funding agencies that publish grants, partner with institutions, and manage events.
Verified NGO/funding agency profile with mission statement and funded areas
Post funding opportunities with eligibility, grant quantum, and deadlines
Organise workshops, capacity-building events, and awareness programmes
Post procurement needs for programme materials, equipment, and services
Connect with institutions and researchers for collaborative programme delivery
Cannot post academic job vacancies or PhD positions
Payment integration deferred to v2.0 (grant disbursements not handled in-platform)
Publish funding calls → researchers/faculty receive push notifications
Organise and manage events with online registration
Post procurement requirements → engage verified vendors
Suppliers and service providers of furniture, lab equipment, infrastructure items, and educational materials to institutions.
Registered vendor listing with product/service catalogue, categories, and pricing information
Browse and filter open procurement requirements posted by institutions
Submit competitive quotes directly to institutions through the platform
Communicate with institutions via platform-routed messaging; direct numbers never exposed
View status of submitted quotes, active orders, and completed transactions
Place sponsored listings in the vendor marketplace to increase discoverability
Cannot access personal user profiles, connections, or feed content
Cannot post academic job vacancies or PhD calls
Direct phone numbers are never shown to institutions; all contact through platform masking
Cannot approve or modify institution procurement postings
Build product/service catalogue with descriptions and pricing
Browse open procurement requirements → submit quotes
Communicate with institutions via masked messaging
Track quote outcomes and active order status from dashboard
Internal platform operators responsible for content moderation, account verification, and system oversight.
Review, approve, or reject institutional account registration requests
Review uploaded certificate documents; approve with verified badge or reject with reason
Review AI-flagged articles for AI-generated content, plagiarism, or fake profile signals
Act on AI-flagged fake profile signals; suspend or remove accounts
Review and approve advertiser ad submissions; monitor impressions and click analytics
View, suspend, or deactivate any user or institution account
Access platform-wide engagement, registration, and moderation metrics
Admin panel is accessible exclusively to platform admin accounts
Admin accounts cannot be self-registered — provisioned internally only
Log in to dedicated admin panel (separate route, elevated JWT role)
Process institution approval queue — review submissions daily
Process certificate validation queue — approve/reject within 48-hour SLA
Review AI-flagged content queue — action within defined moderation SLA
Monitor analytics dashboard for platform health and engagement metrics
Institutions, vendors, funding agencies, and publishers who place targeted advertisements on the platform.
Submit advertisements with creative content and target audience parameters (user type, institution type, location)
Define target segments by user role, institution category, and geographic region
Place sponsored posts in the professional feed visible to matching user segments
Purchase banner ad slots served across relevant platform pages
View real-time impression counts, click-through rates, and campaign performance
All ad submissions require platform admin review and approval before going live
Targeting parameters cannot include personally identifiable information
Payment for ad campaigns deferred to v2.0
Submit ad creative and targeting parameters via advertiser portal
Platform admin reviews and approves the ad
Ad goes live and is served to matching user segments
Advertiser monitors impressions and CTR via analytics dashboard
The following documents and sources informed the preparation of this Business Requirement Document:
Original project brief provided by the internship organisation (verbal and written handover, April 2026).
Supplementary BRD notes document (PDF) provided by the internship supervisor, April 2026.
LinkedIn platform — reference benchmark for professional networking UI/UX patterns.
JustDial platform — reference benchmark for call masking and vendor-to-customer routing.
Hugging Face AI Detection API: https://huggingface.co/docs
Business Requirement Document — a formal document describing the business solution requirements for a project.
Role-Based Access Control — a system where access rights are assigned based on user roles (personal, institutional admin, platform admin).
JSON Web Token — a compact, URL-safe token used to securely transmit authentication information between client and server.
Push Notification Service — a third-party service used to deliver real-time push notifications to users.
A telephony technique where the recipient's direct number is hidden from the caller; the call routes through a platform proxy number.
The Link and Learn marketplace module where institutions post procurement requirements and vendors submit product/service offers.
A third-party API integration that analyses submitted articles for AI-generated text, plagiarism, and fake profile signals.
A Quick Response code generated for an event registrant, used for attendance verification at the event venue.
Non-Governmental Organization — a non-profit body operating independently of government, eligible for institutional accounts.
A PHP + MySQL backend stack providing authentication, relational database management, file storage, and server-side business logic.
Version 1.0 is the initial platform release delivered during the internship. Version 2.0 refers to the subsequent phase including payments and native mobile apps.
The following table summarises all user types supported by Link and Learn and their primary capabilities on the platform.
Profile, feed, job applications, event registration, messaging, resume download
All student capabilities + publish journals, apply for PhD/research roles, reference checking
Group profile, post jobs, manage events, procurement requirements, verified institution badge
All institutional capabilities + research collaboration postings, grant announcements
Group profile, post funding calls, event management, vendor procurement
Product/service listings, respond to procurement requirements, masked contact via platform
Full moderation, certificate validation, institution approval, ad management, analytics
Appendix B: Recommended Phase 1 Folder Structure
The following directory structure is recommended for the React project to ensure modularity and scalability across all three phases:
components/ Shared reusable UI components (Navbar, Button, Card)
pages/ One folder per route (Feed, Profile, Jobs, Events, Vendors...)
features/ Business logic per module (auth, messaging, resume, vendors...)
hooks/ Custom React hooks (useAuth, useProfile, useJobs...)
api/ PHP API endpoints and backend scripts (db.php, auth.php, storage.php)
The platform is designed to generate revenue through four primary streams from launch:
Enhanced profile features, priority listing, advanced analytics
Paid placement and featured listings in the vendor marketplace
Fee per job vacancy or call-for-application posted
Institutions hiring faculty, PhD, and research roles
Sponsored posts and banner ads served to matching user segments
